question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (front-runner) under the precondition that a victim has signed an ERC2612 permit for MidnightBundles and also holds a pre-existing ERC20 allowance trigger the sequence: (1) front-run and consume the victim's permit signature, (2) victim calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with the same permit, (3) pullToken silently swallows the permit revert via try/catch and falls through to safeTransferFrom, violating the invariant that a permit signature authorizes exactly one token pull of the specified amount, causing scoped impact: victim's pre-existing allowance is consumed without their intent in the same bundle transaction? Proof idea: fuzz permit deadline/nonce/amount, front-run permit in same block, assert victim's token balance decreases by more than intended.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that they are authorized for a victim-taker and referralFeePct is near WAD-1 trigger the sequence: (1) call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct close to WAD, (2) referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) overflows or exceeds maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets, (3) the final safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) underflows and reverts, violating the invariant that a valid bundle with referralFeePct < WAD must always complete without arithmetic revert, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — tokens pulled from msg.sender are locked in MidnightBundles with no recovery path? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [WAD/2, WAD-1], assert no underflow on refund transfer.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged OPERATOR who is isAuthorized[victim][operator]=true under the precondition that the operator calls Midnight.multicall trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = setIsAuthorized(newMaliciousContract, true, victim), (2) multicall call[1] = take(offer, ..., taker=victim, ...) where the take increases victim's debt, violating the invariant that authorization delegation must not be composable with debt-increasing actions in a single atomic transaction without victim's per-action consent, causing scoped impact: victim's debt increases and a new unauthorized operator is installed in one transaction? Proof idea: unit test multicall([setIsAuthorized, take]) and assert victim's isAuthorized state and debt both changed atomically.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.flashLoan + Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under the precondition that they have a valid offer and a flash loan callback contract trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = flashLoan([loanToken], [amount], attackerCallback, data), (2) attackerCallback.onFlashLoan reenters Midnight.multicall with call[0] = take(offer, ...) using flash-loaned tokens as payer, (3) flash loan repayment is satisfied by the take's token flow, violating the invariant that flash loan repayment must come from the callback's own funds and not from a nested take's token transfer, causing scoped impact: attacker takes an offer and receives credit/debt without paying net assets? Proof idea: implement reentrant flash loan callback that calls multicall([take(...)]) and assert net token balance of Midnight is unchanged.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under the precondition that referralFeePct > 0 and the borrower's debt equals exactly D trigger the sequence: (1) call repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets=D, onBehalf, permit, withdrawals, receiver, referralFeePct, referralFeeRecipient), (2) units = assets - referralFeeAssets < D so repay(market, units, onBehalf) only partially repays debt, (3) withdrawCollateral checks isHealthy with remaining debt D - units > 0, (4) health check fails and the entire bundle reverts, violating the invariant that a user who pays assets >= D in a repay-and-withdraw bundle should be able to fully exit their position, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — user cannot exit position via bundle even with sufficient payment? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct and debt amount, assert that assets = D always allows full exit.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.setConsumed + Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged MAKER under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] < maxUnits trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) to cancel all offers in group, (2) multicall call[1] = take(offer with same group, ...) where the take increments consumed[maker][group] += units, (3) the require(newConsumed <= offer.maxUnits) check passes because consumed was set to type(uint256).max and the addition overflows to a small value in unchecked arithmetic, violating the invariant that a cancelled offer (consumed = type(uint256).max) cannot be filled, causing scoped impact: offer replay — a cancelled offer is filled after cancellation in the same multicall? Proof idea: check if consumed[maker][group] + units overflows uint256 when consumed = type(uint256).max.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget + Midnight.take health check] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under the precondition that the market has multiple collateral assets trigger the sequence: (1) supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget supplies collateral at index 0, (2) take(offer.buy=true) increases seller's debt, (3) isHealthy at end of take iterates collateralBitmap but the newly supplied collateral at index 0 was not yet reflected in collateralBitmap because oldCollateral was 0 and setBit was called, (4) health check passes but the bitmap state is inconsistent with actual collateral, violating the invariant that collateralBitmap must exactly reflect all nonzero collateral entries after any state-changing sequence, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — health check uses stale bitmap? Proof idea: supply collateral, sell, assert collateralBitmap bit is set and isHealthy returns correct value.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.liquidate + Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under the precondition that a borrower is unhealthy and the liquidation lock uses transient storage trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = take(offer where borrower is seller, ...) which sets liquidationLocked[id][borrower]=true via tExchange, (2) during the take's sellerCallback, the callback reenters multicall with call[0] = liquidate(borrower, ...), (3) liquidationLocked returns true so liquidate reverts with NotLiquidatable, (4) after take completes the lock is cleared, (5) multicall call[1] = liquidate(borrower) now succeeds, violating the invariant that the liquidation lock must prevent liquidation for the entire duration of the take's callback execution, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a position that should be protected during take callbacks? Proof idea: implement sellerCallback that reenters multicall([liquidate(...)]) and assert it reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral + ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that two offers share the same group and the bundler iterates them sequentially trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits reads consumed[maker][group] before any take, (2) take offer[0] increments consumed[maker][group] by units0, (3) consumableUnits for offer[1] still uses the pre-take consumed value (stale read), (4) unitsToTake for offer[1] is computed as if offer[0] was not filled, (5) take offer[1] pushes consumed[maker][group] past maxUnits, violating the invariant that the group budget cannot be exceeded across multiple offers in the same bundle, causing scoped impact: offer overfill — maker's group budget is exceeded in a single bundle transaction? Proof idea: create two offers sharing a group, bundle-take both, assert consumed[maker][group] <= maxUnits.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.withdraw + Midnight.repay] Can an unprivileged LENDER under the precondition that withdrawable < credit trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = repay(market, units, onBehalf=borrower, callback=0, data='') which increases marketState[id].withdrawable by units, (2) multicall call[1] = withdraw(market, units, onBehalf=lender, receiver) which decreases withdrawable by units, (3) the repay in call[0] is for a different user's debt but the withdraw in call[1] benefits the lender, violating the invariant that a lender can only withdraw assets that correspond to repaid debt in the same market, causing scoped impact: direct loss — lender withdraws assets funded by a third-party repayment without the repayer's intent? Proof idea: multicall([repay(borrower), withdraw(lender)]) and assert lender's token balance increases by repaid amount.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken (ERC2612 path) + safeTransferFrom sequencing] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under the precondition that a victim has signed a permit for amount X and has a pre-existing allowance of Y > X trigger the sequence: (1) attacker calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with maxBuyerAssets = X + Y, (2) pullToken calls permit(victim, bundler, X+Y, ...) which fails because the permit was for X only, (3) try/catch swallows the revert, (4) safeTransferFrom(token, victim, bundler, X+Y) succeeds using the pre-existing allowance Y plus the permit-granted X, violating the invariant that a permit signature must bound the maximum token pull to the signed amount, causing scoped impact: direct loss — victim loses X+Y tokens instead of X? Proof idea: set pre-existing allowance Y, sign permit for X, call bundle with X+Y, assert victim balance decreases by X+Y.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (buyer path) + Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under the precondition that they have existing debt and collateral trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = take(offer.buy=false, taker=borrower) which reduces borrower's debt via buyerCreditIncrease path (units <= buyerPos.debt so debt decreases), (2) multicall call[1] = withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, borrower, receiver) which calls isHealthy with the now-reduced debt, (3) isHealthy passes because debt was reduced in call[0], (4) collateral is withdrawn, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal health check must reflect the final state after all composed actions, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal — borrower withdraws collateral that was only freed by a same-transaction debt reduction? Proof idea: multicall([take(reduce debt), withdrawCollateral(max)]) and assert health holds post-withdrawal.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget referral fee overflow] Can an unprivileged CALLER under the precondition that referralFeePct is near WAD-1 and targetSellerAssets is near type(uint128).max trigger the sequence: (1) referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) produces a value near type(uint256).max, (2) targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets overflows uint256, (3) the loop condition filledSellerAssets < targetFilledSellerAssets wraps around and the loop runs indefinitely or skips, violating the invariant that targetFilledSellerAssets must always be a valid uint256 greater than targetSellerAssets, causing scoped impact: fund freeze or accounting corruption in the bundle? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD and targetSellerAssets near uint128.max, assert no overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (maturity boundary)] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that block.timestamp == market.maturity trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = take(offer, units, taker=seller) where block.timestamp == market.maturity, (2) the check require(block.timestamp <= offer.market.maturity || sellerDebtIncrease == 0) passes because block.timestamp == maturity (<=), (3) multicall call[1] = take(offer2, units2, taker=seller) where block.timestamp is still == maturity, (4) seller's debt increases twice at exactly maturity, violating the invariant that debt must not increase at or after maturity, causing scoped impact: bad debt — borrower accumulates debt at maturity boundary via multicall? Proof idea: warp to maturity, multicall([take(sell), take(sell)]), assert debt did not increase.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral + try/catch offer skip] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that some offers in the takes array revert trigger the sequence: (1) buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral iterates takes, (2) take(offer[i]) reverts (e.g., ratifier fails), the catch block silently skips it, (3) filledUnits does not reach targetUnits, (4) require(filledUnits == targetUnits) reverts the entire bundle, (5) but the permit was already consumed in pullToken before the loop, violating the invariant that a consumed permit must correspond to a completed token pull, causing scoped impact: permit replay vulnerability — permit is consumed but no tokens are pulled if the bundle reverts? Proof idea: construct a bundle where all offers revert, assert permit nonce is incremented but no transfer occurred.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.setIsAuthorized + Midnight.take (self-authorization)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under the precondition that they have no authorization for a victim trigger the sequence: (1) attacker calls EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized with a valid victim signature to authorize attacker, (2) in the same transaction, attacker calls Midnight.multicall([take(..., taker=victim, ...), setIsAuthorized(attacker, false, victim)]), (3) the take executes with attacker authorized, then authorization is revoked, violating the invariant that authorization state must be consistent before and after a composed multicall, causing scoped impact: unauthorized take — attacker takes on behalf of victim and immediately removes evidence of authorization? Proof idea: authorize, multicall([take, deauthorize]), assert victim's position changed and attacker is no longer authorized.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken (Permit2 path) + multicall sequencing] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under the precondition that a victim has signed a Permit2 signature for amount X trigger the sequence: (1) attacker calls supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with Permit2 permit for collateral, (2) IPermit2.permitTransferFrom is called (no try/catch), (3) in the same outer transaction, attacker calls the same bundle function again with the same Permit2 nonce, (4) Permit2 reverts on the second call due to nonce reuse, (5) the second bundle call reverts but the first already transferred collateral and increased victim's debt, violating the invariant that a Permit2 signature must be atomic with the full bundle execution, causing scoped impact: partial state — victim's debt increased but second bundle's collateral supply was not completed? Proof idea: call bundle twice with same Permit2 nonce, assert second call reverts and first call's state is consistent.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.liquidate + Midnight.take (bad debt sequencing)] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under the precondition that a borrower has bad debt trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = liquidate(borrower, seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) to realize bad debt and update lossFactor, (2) multicall call[1] = take(offer, units, taker=lender) where lender has credit, (3) _updatePosition is called for lender in take, applying the new lossFactor from call[0], (4) lender's credit is slashed in the same transaction as the bad debt realization, violating the invariant that bad debt slashing must be applied consistently and not double-applied if the lender interacts in the same multicall, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — lender's credit is slashed and then further reduced by the take's credit accounting? Proof idea: multicall([liquidate(bad debt), take(lender)]), assert lender's credit equals expected post-slash value.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral + collateral withdrawal after take] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that the taker has existing collateral and debt trigger the sequence: (1) buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral takes sell offers, reducing taker's debt (buying units reduces debt first), (2) after the take loop, collateralWithdrawals are processed, (3) withdrawCollateral calls isHealthy with the post-take debt, (4) the health check passes because debt was reduced, (5) collateral is withdrawn, (6) but the taker's remaining debt is still nonzero and the withdrawn collateral was needed for health, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must leave the position healthy with the final debt state, causing scoped impact: unhealthy position — taker withdraws too much collateral after debt reduction? Proof idea: fuzz units taken and collateral withdrawn, assert isHealthy(post-withdrawal) is always true.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (payer confusion)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under the precondition that they control a buyerCallback contract trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = take(offer.buy=true, takerCallback=attackerContract, ...) where payer = buyerCallback = attackerContract, (2) attackerContract.onBuy is called, attacker reenters multicall with call[0] = take(offer2, ...) using a different offer, (3) the inner take's payer is also attackerContract, (4) attackerContract pays for both takes in sequence, (5) the outer take's safeTransferFrom is called after the inner take completes, violating the invariant that each take's token transfer must correspond to exactly one take's accounting update, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — token transfers and credit/debt updates are mismatched across nested takes? Proof idea: implement reentrant buyerCallback, assert token balance equals sum of both takes' buyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.forceApproveMax + multicall sequencing] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under the precondition that MidnightBundles has a residual token allowance for Midnight trigger the sequence: (1) a previous bundle call left MidnightBundles with allowance >= type(uint96).max/2 for Midnight, (2) attacker calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with maxBuyerAssets=0 and an empty takes array, (3) forceApproveMax skips re-approval (allowance already high), (4) pullToken pulls 0 tokens, (5) the bundle completes with filledUnits=0 and require(filledUnits == targetUnits=0) passes, (6) attacker calls withdrawCollateral on behalf of taker using the bundler's residual allowance, violating the invariant that MidnightBundles must not hold residual token balances or allowances exploitable by subsequent callers, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal using bundler's residual state? Proof idea: leave residual allowance, call bundle with 0 target, assert no unauthorized state change.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (reduceOnly flag bypass)] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that a maker has a reduceOnly=true sell offer trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = take(offer.buy=false, offer.reduceOnly=true, units=X) where X <= seller's credit, so sellerDebtIncrease=0 and the reduceOnly check passes, (2) multicall call[1] = take(offer.buy=false, offer.reduceOnly=true, units=Y) where Y > seller's remaining credit after call[0], so sellerDebtIncrease > 0, (3) the reduceOnly check in call[1] fails with MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased, (4) but call[0] already reduced the seller's credit, violating the invariant that a reduceOnly offer must never increase the maker's debt across any sequence of takes, causing scoped impact: offer bug — reduceOnly semantics are violated when credit is depleted by a prior take in the same multicall? Proof idea: multicall([take(reduceOnly, units=credit), take(reduceOnly, units=1)]), assert second take reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget + gate check sequencing] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under the precondition that the market has an enterGate that checks a whitelist trigger the sequence: (1) supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget supplies collateral (no gate check in supplyCollateral), (2) take(offer.buy=true) increases seller's debt, (3) enterGate.canIncreaseDebt(seller) is called and returns false because the gate was updated between collateral supply and take, (4) the take reverts with SellerGatedFromIncreasingDebt, (5) the entire bundle reverts but the collateral supply already happened, violating the invariant that a bundle must be atomic — either all actions succeed or none, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — collateral is locked in Midnight with no debt to justify it? Proof idea: set gate to reject after collateral supply, call bundle, assert collateral is recoverable.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.take (continuous fee sequencing)] Can an unprivileged LENDER under the precondition that a market has a nonzero continuousFee trigger the sequence: (1) multicall call[0] = take(offer.buy=false, taker=lender) which calls _updatePosition(lender) accruing fee up to block.timestamp, (2) multicall call[1] = take(offer.buy=false, taker=lender) which calls _updatePosition(lender) again with lastAccrual=block.timestamp, (3) the second _updatePosition accrues zero fee (accrualEnd - lastAccrual = 0), (4) but buyerPendingFeeIncrease in call[1] is computed with timeToMaturity and continuousFee, adding new pendingFee, violating the invariant that pendingFee must equal the integral of continuousFee over the lender's credit lifetime, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — pendingFee is double-counted or under-accrued in same-block multicall? Proof idea: multicall([take, take]) in same block, assert pendingFee equals expected value.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral + TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged TAKER under the precondition that the settlement fee changes between the TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits call and the actual take trigger the sequence: (1) buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral calls touchMarket to cache settlement fees, (2) TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits reads settlementFee(id, timeToMaturity) to compute unitsToTake, (3) between the view call and the take, block.timestamp advances (e.g., via a callback), changing timeToMaturity and thus settlementFee, (4) the actual take uses a different settlementFee, so buyerAssets != targetFilledBuyerAssets, (5) require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets) reverts, violating the invariant that a bundle with a fixed targetBuyerAssets must always complete if offers are available, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — tokens pulled but bundle reverts due to fee drift? Proof idea: advance time in a callback between fee read and take, assert bundle reverts.
